decks.notes: The primary objective of this UW Control deck is to utilize a high number of control spells and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan and outvalue them in the long game. The deck includes a variety of counterspells, removal, and card draw options to maintain control of the board and resources. Yorion, Sky Nomad is included as the companion to provide additional value and flexibility in game plan. The deck aims to stabilize the board, draw out the game, and eventually overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and card advantage.

This deck focuses on using a combination of cheap spells and aggressive creatures to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. By utilizing cards like Proft's Eidetic Memory and Agatha's Soul Cauldron to generate card advantage, the deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. With a mix of direct damage spells like Lightning Axe and Torch the Tower, along with versatile creatures like Vivi Ornitier and Marauding Mako, the deck aims to close out games quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This Golgari Aggro deck focuses on generating value by sacrificing creatures with cards like Witch's Oven and Cauldron Familiar, while also utilizing cards like Scavenger's Talent and Dredger's Insight to keep the pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of creatures and removal spells like Fatal Push, while also having the flexibility to tutor for specific cards with Traverse the Ulvenwald. With a mix of efficient creatures and disruptive spells, this deck is designed to maintain board presence and grind out wins against various strategies.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up a board presence with resilient creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Graveyard Trespasser. It incorporates powerful removal spells such as Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse provides additional value by recurring creatures from the graveyard. The mana base is carefully curated to support the deck's game plan, allowing for consistent and effective plays in each match.

The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to have a fast and aggressive start by playing low-cost creatures like Novice Inspector and Ornithopter, while using cards like Portable Hole and Gleeful Demolition to remove any blockers or threats. The deck aims to quickly build a wide board of creatures and overwhelm the opponent with cards like Venerated Loxodon and Cosmogrand Zenith. The mana base is optimized for consistent and fast plays, allowing the deck to maintain pressure on the opponent and secure a victory through efficient aggression.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board with a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures to outvalue and outlast your opponent. Yorion, Sky Nomad acts as a companion to provide additional value and card advantage. The deck includes various utility lands to help stabilize the mana base and provide extra synergy with the control elements. With a combination of counter spells, removal, and powerful threats, this Dimir Control deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast opponents in a longer game.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful board presence using Greasefang and Parhelion II, supported by cards like Concealed Courtyard, Multiversal Passage, and Esika's Chariot. It also includes cards like Bitter Triumph and Thundering Broodwagon to control the board and prevent opponent's strategies. The deck has a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to provide a versatile and adaptive gameplay style, aiming to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and strategic spellcasting.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost spells and creatures to chip away at the opponent's life total while maintaining control of the board with removal spells. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, while using interactive spells like Spell Pierce and Reckless Rage to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck's mana base is optimized to provide the right colors of mana consistently, allowing the player to cast their spells on curve and pressure the opponent early in the game.

The deck revolves around assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by using cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to generate value and protect the combo pieces. Thoughtseize and Guardian of New Benalia help control the opponent's hand and board, while Iron-Shield Elf provides protection for key creatures. The deck also has removal in the form of Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to deal with threats. With careful planning and sequencing, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and win through the powerful Greasefang Parhelion combo.

The key concept of this deck is to aggressively control the battlefield through a combination of burn spells, bounce effects, and card draw, while utilizing prowess creatures to deal damage quickly. By efficiently removing opponent's threats and disrupting their game plan, the deck aims to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent to quickly close out the game. The deck also focuses on tempo plays to keep the opponent on the back foot and capitalizes on the prowess triggers to maximize damage output.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low cost creatures and burn spells. It prioritizes putting pressure on the opponent early in the game, making it difficult for them to stabilize and come back. The deck aims to finish the game quickly before the opponent can set up their own strategies. It utilizes a mix of creatures with haste and direct damage spells to ensure a fast and efficient win condition.

The main focus of this Izzet Control deck is to maintain control of the game by utilizing a combination of efficient removal spells, card draw, and counter spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage through spells like Pop Quiz and Accumulate Wisdom, while also having answers to threats with cards like Divide by Zero and Combustion Technique. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Thor, God of Thunder and Gran-Gran helps close out games once the opponent's resources are depleted. The mana base is optimized for consistent access to both blue and red mana sources.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board while utilizing powerful mono black cards to disrupt their strategy. Cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Duress help to strip away the opponent's resources, while creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Gifted Aetherborn provide pressure and life gain. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and The Meathook Massacre to close out the game. With a mix of removal, disruption, and threats, this deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent in a long game.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells in order to deal damage quickly and efficiently. By utilizing creatures like Emberheart Challenger and Monastery Swiftspear, along with spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to control the board and apply pressure to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Kumano, Faces Kakkazan and Robber of the Rich adds additional utility and versatility to the deck, allowing for various strategic options depending on the matchup.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and disruptive creatures. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while generating card advantage and setting up for big plays with cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Yorion, Sky Nomad. The deck also includes a variety of lands to support its strategy, including utility lands like Otawara, Hive of the Eye Tyrant, and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth. The overall goal is to outlast the opponent and grind out a victory through superior card advantage and control.

The key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of low-cost, evasive Spirit creatures and tempo-controlling spells. By using cards like Essence Flux and Daydream to protect and recur creatures, while also utilizing mana ramp with Mox Amber and Springleaf Drum, the deck aims to establish early board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game. With a focus on flying creatures and protection spells, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and control the battlefield, ultimately leading to a quick victory through aggressive tempo play.
